title: VIX & S&P 500 correlation in `report.md`
why: Tells you whether the strategy is a hidden beta play. A "market-neutral" XAU strategy with `corr(returns, S&P) = -0.4` is actually a risk-off bet, not alpha. Currently missing from the report.
scope: 2 numbers + 1 paragraph in [pipeline/reporting.py](pipeline/reporting.py). Macro frame already loads VIXCLS; need to add SPY/^GSPC to the FRED list or compute from existing data.

history.reason: XS reporting hygiene from maxdama alpha checklist — quick win after the Phase 5 infra cluster. Tells us if a XAU strategy is a hidden risk-off beta play rather than alpha.

Added compute_benchmark_correlations to pipeline/reporting.py (Pearson corr of daily strategy PnL vs VIX + S&P 500 daily returns; maxdama 4.10 hidden-beta check). SP500 added to macro_fetch.FRED_SERIES (hand-picked feature selection in build_macro_features means it is fetched/cached but NOT a model feature — verified). Wired into scripts/run_xau_d1.py: benchmark levels loaded from cache/fred/ (graceful None when absent), emitted to summary.json[benchmark_correlations] + a new report.md section. Diagnostic-only, never gates the stack decision. Tests: tests/test_benchmark_correlations.py (6 cases: +1/-1 recovery, low-overlap None, empty pnl, tz-naive alignment, sparse same-day aggregation, NaN-on-zero-variance guard). Updated 2 macro_fetch test fixtures for the new series. 545/545 tests pass.
feat(phase5): close 7 tickets â€” Loop A/H4 infra hardening + reporting
fix(B0003): keep benchmark-only SP500 out of feature-critical FRED_SERIES
chore(backlog): record commit-sync provenance for B0003/B0032
